methods.name: fund_due_cashflows

args.name: max_event_count
args.desc: Maximum number of due cash events to process in this call.

returns.desc: Funding result with reserved interest, reserved principal, and event count.

methods.desc: Reserve due ACTUS cash events into the escrow-style claim ledger.

events.name: ExecutionEvent
events.desc: Non-normative receipt for on-chain execution of a scheduled ACTUS event.

methods.name: claim_due_cashflows

args.name: holding_address
args.desc: Holder whose position should be settled and claimed.

args.name: payment_info
args.desc: Opaque settlement context returned in the result.

returns.desc: Claim result with realized interest, principal, and passthrough context.

methods.desc: Withdraw the holder's already funded ACTUS cash entitlements.

methods.name: transfer_set_schedule

args.name: open_date
args.desc: Inclusive UNIX timestamp at which transfers may start.

args.name: closure_date
args.desc: Exclusive UNIX timestamp at which transfers stop.

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the schedule update.

methods.desc: Configure the secondary-market transfer window.

methods.name: primary_distribution

returns.desc: Remaining undistributed unit balance.

methods.desc: Reserve units for a holder before the initial exchange activates issuance.

methods.name: transfer

args.name: units
args.desc: Number of active units to move.

returns.desc: Number of units transferred.

methods.desc:
Transfer active units after settling both counterparties to the current cursor.
Returns the number of units moved. The ACTUS kernel no longer persists a nominal per-unit value on-chain, so transfer results are unit-based.

methods.name: account_suspension

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the suspension update.

methods.desc: Set the suspension status of one account.

methods.name: account_open

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the account opening.

methods.desc: Open a contract account with a new position and register its payment address.

methods.name: account_update_payment_address

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the account update.

methods.desc: Update the payment address of an account.

methods.name: account_get_position

returns.desc: Account position record.

methods.desc: Get the full accounting position for one holder.

methods.name: contract_create

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of contract creation.

methods.desc: Create a new D-ASA contract and set the arranger role.

methods.name: contract_config

args.name: prospectus
args.desc: Prospectus metadata stored for reference only.

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of contract configuration.

methods.desc:
Partial contract configuration with: normalized ACTUS terms, initial kernel
state (pre-IED), prospectus (informational only). The contract stays inactive until all schedule pages are configured next.

methods.name: contract_schedule

args.name: schedule_page_index
args.desc: Zero-based page index within the boxed schedule.

args.name: is_last_page
args.desc: Whether this page finalizes the schedule upload.

args.name: schedule_page
args.desc: Normalized schedule entries for the requested page.

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the schedule upload.

methods.desc:
Upload one normalized schedule page.
The configuration operation is considered complete only when the last page is stored. At that point `schedule_entry_count` is finalized and the contract moves to `STATUS_PENDING_IED`.

methods.name: contract_execute_ied

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the `IED` execution.

methods.desc:
Execute the first due `IED` entry and activate the contract.
`IED` is the one non-cash event that moves the app from `STATUS_PENDING_IED` to `STATUS_ACTIVE`.

events.name: ExecutionEvent
events.desc: Non-normative receipt for on-chain execution of a scheduled ACTUS event.

methods.name: apply_non_cash_event

args.name: event_id
args.desc: Expected global schedule event identifier at the current cursor.

args.name: payload
args.desc: Observed payload used for append-on-execute and/or rate resets.

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the non-cash event application.

methods.desc:
Apply the next due non-cash event, optionally appending one first.
Observed payloads are appended before execution when flagged. Rate reset events require the interest oracle because they can change the running nominal rate; other non-cash events stay arranger-controlled.

events.name: ExecutionEvent
events.desc: Non-normative receipt for on-chain execution of a scheduled ACTUS event.

methods.name: append_observed_cash_event

args.name: payload
args.desc: Observed cash-event payload to append after the current tail.

returns.desc: UNIX timestamp of the append operation.

methods.desc: Append an arranger-authorized observed cash event to the schedule tail.

methods.name: contract_get_state

returns.desc: Current kernel state snapshot.

methods.desc: Return a readonly snapshot of the generic ACTUS kernel state.

methods.name: contract_get_next_due_event

returns.desc: Next due schedule entry, or an all-zero sentinel if the contract ended.

methods.desc: Return the next boxed schedule entry, or a zeroed sentinel if ended.

methods.desc: Update D-ASA application.
